text.source_text: अत्राह — योऽयमात्मविषयः प्रश्नः — ‘ येयं प्रेते विचिकित्सा मनुष्येऽस्ति ’ इति , किं स एवायम् ‘ अन्यत्र धर्मादन्यत्राधर्मात् ’ इति पुनरनुकृष्यते , किं वा ततोऽन्योऽयमपूर्वः प्रश्न उत्थाप्यत इति । किं चातः ? स ए वायं प्रश्नः पुनरनुकृष्यत इति यद्युच्येत , तदा द्वयोरात्मविषययोः प्रश्नयोरेकतापत्तेरग्निविषय आत्मविषयश्च द्वावेव प्रश्नावित्यतो न वक्तव्यं त्रयाणां प्रश्नोपन्यासाविति । अथान्योऽयमपूर्वः प्रश्न उत्थाप्यत इत्युच्येत , ततो यथैव वरप्रदानव्यतिरेकेण प्रश्नकल्पनायामदोषः ; एवं प्रश्नव्यतिरेकेणापि प्रधानोपन्यासकल्पनायामदोषः स्यादिति ॥
text.transliteration_iast: atrāha — yo'yamātmaviṣayaḥ praśnaḥ — ‘ yeyaṃ prete vicikitsā manuṣye'sti ’ iti , kiṃ sa evāyam ‘ anyatra dharmādanyatrādharmāt ’ iti punaranukṛṣyate , kiṃ vā tato'nyo'yamapūrvaḥ praśna utthāpyata iti | kiṃ cātaḥ ? sa e vāyaṃ praśnaḥ punaranukṛṣyata iti yadyucyeta , tadā dvayorātmaviṣayayoḥ praśnayorekatāpatteragniviṣaya ātmaviṣayaśca dvāveva praśnāvityato na vaktavyaṃ trayāṇāṃ praśnopanyāsāviti | athānyo'yamapūrvaḥ praśna utthāpyata ityucyeta , tato yathaiva varapradānavyatirekeṇa praśnakalpanāyāmadoṣaḥ ; evaṃ praśnavyatirekeṇāpi pradhānopanyāsakalpanāyāmadoṣaḥ syāditi ||

translation.literal_translation: An objection is raised: regarding the self-related question 'this doubt when a person has died,' is that very question drawn forward again in 'beyond dharma, beyond adharma,' or is this a different, previously unraised question? What follows from this? If the same question is repeated, then two self-questions collapse into one, leaving only two questions total (fire and self), so it is improper to speak of three question-expositions. If, however, this is a new and different question, then just as there is no fault in positing a question outside strict boon-allocation, there would likewise be no fault in positing an independent mention of pradhana even apart from an explicit question.
translation.technical_translation: Purvapaksha presses a structural dilemma: either the later 'anyatra dharmat...' query is anukarsa (continuation) of the earlier post-mortem self-doubt, or it is an apurva-praśna (new question). On the first horn, the triadic question-count collapses; on the second, the interpretive gatekeeping principle ('only explicitly asked topics may be taught') weakens, reopening room for a pradhana insertion. The objection thus targets the consistency of the tri-question constraint used against Sankhya import.
translation.interpretive_note: Dialectical pressure point: if question-boundary is unstable, topic-boundary is unstable.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: This block is intentionally rendered as an argumentative dilemma rather than a settled claim.
Terms like anukarsa/apurva are translated functionally; you may prefer explicit Sanskrit retention in final curation.
